message:
fix(indexer): self-heal orphan FK rows at indexer startup (cass#202)

A `Connection` dropped mid-transaction (the `drop_close` warning emitted
by frankensqlite's `Drop` impl) can leave child rows persisted without a
matching parent — `messages` referencing a `conversation_id` that does
not exist, and the `message_metrics`/`token_usage`/`snippets` cascades
that follow. With `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON`, every subsequent indexer
pass then trips `FOREIGN KEY constraint failed` on the next write, the
session never gets marked indexed, and the pending backlog grows
without bound (cass#202: 7,982 pending sessions, 22 GB RAM, 400% CPU
on every session close until manual cleanup).

Add `FrankenStorage::cleanup_orphan_fk_rows` and call it from
`run_index` before any indexing work begins (skipped on full rebuild
since the canonical DB will be replaced anyway). For each child table
in `ORPHAN_FK_TABLES`, count rows whose FK parent has gone missing,
then `DELETE` them in a single transaction. The static SQL list keeps
the predicate auditable and prevents identifier injection. Idempotent:
a clean database is a no-op.

This is defense in depth — the underlying transaction discipline still
needs hardening (drop_close should already be impossible when commits
are properly bounded), but a self-healing pass at indexer startup
ensures one bad commit cannot poison every future run. The WARN log
on cleanup keeps the upstream condition visible so the root cause
stays on the radar.

Closes #202

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
